articles.title: Kuwait condemns what it calls Iranian attack on power, water desalination facility
articles.summary: Kuwait condemns what it calls Iranian attack on power, water desalination facility The Kuwaiti foreign ministry condemned on Sunday what it described as an Iranian attack on a power generation and water desalination facility, calling it a direct strike on vital civilian infrastructure.  The repeated targeting of civilian facilities amounted to a dangerous escalation and violated international law, the UN Charter and UN Security Council Resolution 2817, the ministry said in a statement.  It said Iran bore full legal and moral responsibility for the attack and its consequences. "The Secretary commended the courage of the Government of Lebanon, under President Aoun’s leadership,” State Department spokesperson Tommy Pigott said in a statement. He also praised the government’s efforts to disarm Hezbollah.

articles.title: Iran executes two men over killing of police officers
articles.summary: Iran executes two men over killing of police officers Iran has executed two men convicted of killing four police officers during anti-government unrest in the central city of Isfahan in January, according to the judiciary-affiliated Mizan news agency. Mizan identified the men as Erfan Esfandiyari and Golmohammad Mohammadi, saying their death sentences were carried out after court proceedings. They were convicted of attacking the officers, tying them to a road sign, assaulting them with stones, dousing them with petrol and setting them on fire. articles.summary: Jordan said no evacuation of Aqaba airport or seaport after US warning Jordan denied reports that Aqaba airport and seaport had been evacuated after the US embassy warned Americans of a “credible threat”. Jordanian government spokesman Mohammad al-Momani told the Petra news agency that “relevant authorities have not issued any evacuation orders at the Aqaba airport or seaport”. Momani said authorities had not recorded any potential threats to Aqaba over the previous several hours. Both the airport and seaport were operating normally, he added.

articles.summary: Trump says he ‘couldn’t care less’ about Iran leaving nuclear agreement US President Donald Trump said he “couldn’t care less” after Iran announced it would no longer abide by the US-Iran memorandum of understanding (MoU) signed last month. Speaking in a brief phone interview with US cable network NewsNation, Trump reiterated that the US’s main objective remains “never allowing Iran to have a nuclear weapon”. Washington and Tehran signed the MoU in mid-June in an effort to end the war launched by the US and Israel in February.
